g.title: Large Front Wood Panel
g.desc: This is a large, flat, laser-cut wood piece shaped like a wide, upside-down 'U' with a slightly angled, house-like peak at the top. Along its outer top and side edges, you can feel several small protruding rectangular tabs and T-shaped cutouts. The inner cutout is a broad, rectangular space with rounded top corners.

g.title: Three 1M Ohm Resistors
g.desc: Locate three small electronic resistors. Each component has a tiny, smooth cylindrical body, about the size of a grain of rice, with a very thin, flexible metal wire extending from each end. Tactually, you can identify them by feeling for the small hard center and the thin leads, which are often bent into a 'U' shape for assembly.

g.title: Single Rectangular Solar Panel
g.desc: This component is a single, large rectangular solar panel. You can identify it by its thin, flat shape and very smooth, glass-like top surface. It has four slightly rounded corners. On the back, you will find electrical connection points or wires.

g.title: Half-Inch Number Four-Forty Screws
g.desc: You should have four small brass screws. Each screw is half an inch long. You can identify them by their thin, ridged cylindrical shaft and a wider, rounded head with a slot for a screwdriver. Use your fingernail to feel the tiny ridges of the threads along the shaft.

g.title: Four Hexagonal Hardware Nuts
g.desc: Identify these four small brass pieces by their six flat outer sides, which form a hexagon shape. Each nut has a circular hole in the center with tiny internal ridges called threads. They are symmetrical, so they can be used with either flat face pointing up. You will need four of these for assembly.

g.title: Large Roof Panel Component
g.desc: A large rectangular wooden panel with rounded corners. It features a prominent oval cutout in the center, flanked by two small circular screw holes. There are also four narrow, vertical rectangular slots, one positioned near each of the four corners along the longer sides of the panel.

g.title: Side Panel with Hook Tabs
g.desc: This large rectangular wooden panel has a wide rectangular cutout in the center of its bottom edge. On both the left and right vertical edges, there are two hook-shaped tabs that curve downward. To orient the piece, place the side with the large gap at the bottom. The top edge is a straight, flat line.

g.title: Identification of the Single Electrolytic Capacitor
g.desc: This electronic component is a small, smooth cylinder with two thin metal wire legs protruding from one flat end. You can identify it by its cylindrical body and the two stiff wires. One leg is longer than the other to help you orient it correctly, and there is a stripe on the side of the cylinder marking the negative side.

g.title: Side Panel Piece with Four Window Cutouts
g.desc: This is a flat, 5mm thick laser-cut plywood side panel. On both its left and right vertical edges, there are two protruding hook-shaped tabs, with the hooks pointing downwards. In the center of the piece, you will feel four small square cutouts arranged in a two-by-two grid, resembling a window with four panes.

g.title: Large Wood Back Panel Piece
g.desc: This is a large, flat, laser-cut wood piece labeled 'Back'. Its top edge is angled into a central peak, resembling a simple roofline. In the middle of the piece, you will feel twelve square window cutouts arranged in three separate groups of four. Near the straight left and right side edges, there are two vertical rectangular slots on each side. The sloped top edges also feature several small notches and T-shaped cutouts for connecting other pieces.

g.title: Identifying the Small Roof Panel Component
g.desc: A single rectangular piece of 5mm thick plywood with rounded corners. When held horizontally, you will find a small vertical rectangular cutout near both the left and right short edges. Directly beneath each of these rectangular slots is a small circular hole meant for the brass hardware.
